meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding to overcome no project ideas. An intermediate-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Overcoming 'no project ideas' requires building high-utility micro-SaaS features. User onboarding is the perfect intermediate project because it forces you to master state management, UX flow, and database persistence. By building a modular onboarding engine, you solve a universal engineering pain point while creating a reusable, portfolio-ready asset.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the blinking cursor, paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' paradox, while your peers ship production-grade software. The secret isn't a better idea; it's a better architectural challenge. User onboarding is the crucible where frontend polish meets backend logic.

Stop hunting for the 'next big thing' and start engineering the infrastructure that every SaaS needs. By building a robust, data-driven onboarding flow, you transform a simple tutorial into a high-value module. This project forces you to synthesize complex state, triggers, and analytics—exactly the skills that separate junior learners from senior builders.

steps.title: Architecting the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Define the user journey states (initial, processing, completed) to ensure your onboarding flow is scalable and bug-resistant.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust state machine schema for a multi-step SaaS onboarding flow using TypeScript. Include interfaces for step tracking and user progress persistence.

steps.title: Implementing Persistent Progress Tracking
steps.description: Ensure users can resume their onboarding if they refresh. This requires linking frontend state to a backend database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database migration and API handler for updating user onboarding progress. Ensure it handles atomic updates to prevent race conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement onboarding progress persistence in prisma and nestjs'

steps.title: Designing Dynamic Component Injection
steps.description: Use a registry pattern to load onboarding components dynamically, keeping your main layout clean and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor a React onboarding flow to use a dynamic component registry based on the current step index from the database.

steps.title: Adding Real-Time Analytics Triggers
steps.description: Instrument every step with telemetry to identify where users drop off—crucial for real-world SaaS product success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a middleware hook that sends an event to PostHog every time a user completes an onboarding step, including metadata about the time spent.

steps.title: Final Polish: Error Handling and Edge Cases
steps.description: Build graceful degradation for when the API fails or the user encounters a network error mid-onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write comprehensive error handling logic for the onboarding service, including retry mechanisms for network failures.

pro_tips: Never hardcode onboarding steps; fetch them from a JSON config so you can A/B test variations without redeploying code.
Use optimistic UI updates to make the onboarding feel instantaneous, masking the latency of backend database writes.
Implement a 'Skip' feature early; forcing users through onboarding is a major conversion killer, and tracking skips is valuable data.
Add a 'Progress Saved' indicator that updates via WebSockets; it builds immense user trust in your app's reliability.
Use local storage as a cache for onboarding state to ensure zero-latency transitions between steps before syncing to the server.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, integrate it into a boilerplate SaaS starter kit and document your architectural decisions in a README file to showcase your engineering rigor to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade module that solves a core SaaS problem. Don't let this code sit in a private repo—package it, document it, and deploy it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
